summary: Joe Reis and Matt Housley recorded a discussion about writing a best-selling O'Reilly technical book. The key ingredients are an enormous amount of hard work that most people won't want to do, combined with significant luck that authors often misattribute to their own efforts.
key_points: Writing a best-selling technical book requires extreme hard work
Luck plays a major role in success
Authors tend to falsely attribute success to effort rather than luck
money_quotes: It involves a ton of hard work that you'll probably not want to do, and an insane amount of luck that you'll falsely attribute to your not-so-hard work.
key_insight: Best-selling technical books require both grueling effort most aren't willing to put in and a healthy dose of luck that successful authors often fail to acknowledge.
